commands.review_summary:
The branch rejects Z.AI onboarding command text in interactive and non-interactive API-key setup and adds a Z.AI-specific auth-doctor hint with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +46, Tests +53. Total +99 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: no. not with a high-confidence live Z.AI run in this read-only review. Source inspection shows current main can accept non-empty command text as an API-key prompt value and that static API-key profiles are filtered out of doctor auth issues.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs author or maintainer follow-up for the doctor reachability defect plus contributor-side real behavior proof, so it should stay human-gated rather than enter repair automation.

target.title: fix(zai): reject onboarding command auth profiles
target.body:
﻿Related: #97313

## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where Z.AI users could end up with an auth profile whose stored API key is the onboarding command text, such as `openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global`, causing provider calls to use that command string as the bearer token instead of the real key.

## Why This Change Was Made

The fix keeps the repair narrow to the Z.AI auth boundary: Z.AI interactive and non-interactive auth now reject onboarding command text before it can be stored in `zai:default`, and auth doctor guidance identifies existing Z.AI profiles that already contain that bad command value. This does not change sub-agent fallback behavior or the general auth profile precedence rules.

## User Impact

Users get an immediate setup error if they paste an OpenClaw onboarding command where a Z.AI API key is expected, and existing polluted `zai:default` profiles receive a clearer re-authentication hint instead of repeatedly failing as an opaque Z.AI 401.

## Evidence

Red/green proof:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\vitest.CMD run extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts --reporter=verbose
```

Before the fix, the new regression tests failed because Z.AI returned a profile with `key: "openclaw onboard --auth-choice zai-coding-global"` and doctor returned an empty hint. After the fix: 2 files passed, 19 tests passed.

Additional checks:

```text
.\node_modules\.bin\oxfmt.CMD --check extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts
.\node_modules\.bin\oxlint.CMD extensions/zai/index.ts extensions/zai/index.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/doctor.ts src/agents/auth-profiles.doctor.test.ts
git diff --check
```

All passed.

Validation note: I do not have a live Z.AI / GLM-5.2 credential, so this PR proves the local auth-profile root cause with seeded/plugin tests rather than a live network call.
target.author: lzy3538
